channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines curated by AI

item.title: 🇮🇷✈️ Iran Suspends Flights at Tehran Airport Amid Escalation
item.description: Iran civil aviation authority suspended all flights at Tehran International Airport until further notice, as regional tensions spike following Iran missile launches against Israel.

item.title: 🇨🇳☢️ China Expands Nuclear Arsenal to 620 Warheads: SIPRI
item.description: Swedish think-tank SIPRI reports China has increased its nuclear warheads to 620 and may be increasing deployment as the New Start treaty expiry ends arms control efforts.

item.title: 🇽🇰🗳️ Kosovo PM Kurti Wins Snap Election But Lacks Majority
item.description: Albin Kurtis Vetevendosje party won nearly 44% of the vote in Kosovo parliamentary election, but fell short of a majority needed to end the ongoing political crisis.

item.title: 🇦🇲🗳️ Armenia Votes: Pashinyan Seeks Mandate for Westward Shift
item.description: Armenians head to polls in parliamentary elections that could determine whether PM Pashinyan gets a mandate strong enough to reorient the country toward Europe amid tensions with Russia.

item.title: 🇬🇧🇮🇷 UK Foreign Minister Calls for De-escalation Between Iran and Israel
item.description: UK Foreign Minister Yvette Cooper urged both Iran and Israel to show restraint and de-escalate after Iran renewed missile attacks on Israel.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇮🇱 Iran Fires Missiles at Israel, Violating Ceasefire Agreement
item.description: Iran launched ballistic missiles at Israel for the first time since the April ceasefire, marking a major escalation. Israel's military confirmed the attack, while Iran's state broadcaster acknowledged the strikes.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇰🇵 Xi Jinping Visits North Korea as China Seeks to Counter US Influence
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping arrived in Pyongyang for a state visit, declaring China's unwavering policy to upgrade ties with North Korea. The summit signals Beijing's strategic focus on regional security amid tensions with Washington.

item.title: 🇪🇺🇺🇦 European Leaders Urge Urgent Action to Bolster Ukraine's Missile Defenses
item.description: The leaders of the UK, France, Germany and Ukraine met to discuss ramping up weapons production to counter Russian ballistic missiles, as Zelensky warned Ukraine is close to developing its own ballistic missile capability.

item.title: 🛢️ OPEC+ Approves Fourth Oil Output Hike Despite Hormuz Crisis
item.description: OPEC+ agreed to increase oil production targets for the fourth consecutive month, even as the US-Iran conflict continues to block shipments through the Strait of Hormuz, affecting several member states' ability to pump more.

item.title: 🇫🇷 France Warns of New Sanctions on Israeli Settlers Within Days
item.description: France's foreign minister announced that additional sanctions on Israeli settlers could be imposed in coming days, protesting the escalation of illegal settlements in the West Bank and rising settler violence against Palestinians.

item.title: 🇦🇲 Armenia's Pro-Western Party Leads Election as Pashinyan Defies Russia
item.description: Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan's Civil Contract party is leading with 54% in Armenia's parliamentary election, a shift away from Russian influence toward the West.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Rejects US Plan to Use Frozen Assets for Gulf Allies' Damages
item.description: Tehran dismissed Washington's plan to use Iranian assets to compensate Gulf states for war damages, calling it unacceptable and warning regional governments.

item.title: 🇵🇪 Peru Heads to Runoff as Sánchez Faces Fujimori in Polarized Race
item.description: Peruvians vote in a presidential runoff between Roberto Sánchez and Keiko Fujimori, with EU observers deployed as the country faces deep political divisions.

item.title: 🇽🇰 Kosovo's Kurti Wins Snap Election But Lacks Majority to End Crisis
item.description: Prime Minister Albin Kurti's party won the most votes in Kosovo's third election in a year, but fell short of a majority amid a protracted political crisis.

item.title: 🇨🇦 Canada's Carney Heads to Ireland, France Before G7 Summit
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney will deepen ties with European partners before attending the G7 summit in Evian-les-Bains, with focus on reducing global tensions.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Launches Missile Barrage at Israel, First Since Ceasefire
item.description: Iran fired missiles at Israel in retaliation for Israeli strikes on Beirut, marking the first such attack since the Middle East ceasefire. Israel's air defenses are actively intercepting the incoming threats.

item.title: 🇳🇵🇮🇳 Nepal FM Signals Diplomatic Approach to India Border Row
item.description: Nepal Foreign Minister Shisir Khanal said no boundary is too complex if tackled with an open heart, signaling a diplomatic push to resolve territorial disputes with India and strengthen bilateral ties.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇰🇵 Xi Jinping arrives in Pyongyang for summit with Kim Jong Un
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping visits North Korea as Kim Jong Un projects confidence with firm Russian ties and nuclear arsenal, showing little appetite for engagement with Washington.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇬🇧 US considers buying Chagos Islands from Mauritius
item.description: The White House is weighing a plan to purchase the strategically important Chagos Islands from Mauritius, the Telegraph reports.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇫 Central African Republic agrees to accept third-country deportees from US
item.description: The Trump administration strikes a deal with Central African Republic to accept migrants from other countries deported by the US, the latest in a series of opaque agreements with African states.

item.title: 🇺🇦 The Atlantic: Ukraine Not Losing, Russia Not Winning as Momentum Shifts
item.description: Gulf states and others now understand that the Russian narrative is wrong. Ukrainians continue to resist as the war enters a new phase with drone warfare reshaping the front lines.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan Asserts Maritime Rights as Regional Tensions Escalate
item.description: Taiwan emphasized that its rights over territorial waters and exclusive economic zone must not be violated by any country, amid heightened regional tensions in the Taiwan Strait.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇺🇸 CBS News: Iran Accuses US of Ceasefire Violation as Stalemate Continues
item.description: US military reports shooting down four Iranian drones headed toward Strait of Hormuz overnight. Peace talks remain deadlocked as both sides exchange accusations.

item.title: ⚓ Female Navy Officers Fear Career Cap After Hegseth Cuts Women From Promotions
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth removed all women from a Navy promotion list, raising concerns about gender discrimination in the military and impacting career trajectories for female officers.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK Military Tearing Its Hair Out as Labour Dithering Endangers National Security
item.description: Critics condemn Labour government failure to strengthen British Armed Forces while war rages in Europe, with calls for urgent defence investment amid growing Russian threat.
